name: Angular
description: A TypeScript-based web development framework for building enterprise-scale single-page applications

description.value: A TypeScript-based web development framework for building enterprise-scale single-page applications

initial-release.comment: Angular 2 released September 14, 2016 (complete rewrite from AngularJS)

active-maintenance.comment: Regular releases following predictable time-based schedule with LTS support

reactivity-model.comment: Traditionally uses Zone.js dirty checking; Signals introduced in v16, stable in v20; zoneless option available

component-model.comment: Class-based components with @Component decorator; standalone components default since v19

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative templates with data binding and control flow

state-management-approach.comment: Signals provide built-in reactive state; NgRx for complex global state

opinionatedness.comment: Full framework with prescribed patterns for DI, routing, forms, HTTP, and project structure

backward-compatibility.comment: Predictable release schedule with LTS; ng update provides automated migrations

primary-language.comment: Written in and designed for TypeScript; TypeScript is required

typescript-support.comment: Framework is written in TypeScript; TypeScript is the default and recommended

template-syntax.comment: HTML templates with Angular-specific syntax for binding and control flow

styling-approach.comment: Component-scoped styles by default via view encapsulation; supports CSS, SCSS, Less

build-required.comment: Compilation step required; uses Angular CLI for builds

reactive-primitives.comment: Signals (signal, computed, effect) provide reactive primitives since v16

computed-values.comment: computed() function for derived reactive values

two-way-binding.comment: Built-in [(ngModel)] for two-way binding in forms

component-slots.comment: ng-content for content projection with multi-slot support

teleport-portal.comment: CDK Portal for rendering content outside component tree

suspense.comment: @defer block for lazy loading with loading/error states (v17+)

error-boundaries.comment: ErrorHandler service for global error handling; @defer has @error block

context-provide-inject.comment: Powerful dependency injection system is core to Angular

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Template reference variables (#ref) and ViewChild/ElementRef for DOM access

nested-routes.comment: Full support for child routes and router-outlet nesting

route-guards.comment: CanActivate, CanDeactivate, CanMatch, Resolve guards; functional guards since v14

lazy-loading-routes.comment: loadChildren and loadComponent for lazy loading

built-in-state.comment: Signals provide built-in reactive state management

built-in-global-state.comment: Services with Signals provide global state; no separate library needed for simple cases

global-state-library.comment: NgRx Signal Store is the recommended library for complex state; ~1.2kB gzipped

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Angular DevTools browser extension inspects component state and signals

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via NgRx or community libraries

official-cli.comment: Angular CLI (ng) for scaffolding, building, testing, and deployment

browser-devtools.comment: Angular DevTools extension for Chrome and Firefox with component tree, profiler, DI tree

vs-code-extension.comment: Angular Language Service provides IntelliSense, navigation, and refactoring

error-messages.comment: Good error messages with component context; improved significantly in recent versions

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive documentation at angular.dev with guides, API reference, tutorials, and AI tutor

interactive-tutorial.comment: Multiple browser-based playground tutorials including fundamentals and signals

bundle-size-core.comment: ~90 kB gzipped for @angular/core alone; larger than React due to framework completeness

bundle-size-full.comment: ~150 kB gzipped for core + common + router + forms; varies with tree shaking

virtual-dom.comment: Does not use virtual DOM; uses incremental DOM approach with change detection

compile-time-optimization.comment: Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation performs static analysis and template optimization

tree-shaking.comment: Built-in tree shaking removes unused code

code-splitting.comment: Automatic code splitting with lazy loading

lazy-components.comment: @defer block enables lazy loading of components (v17+)

streaming-ssr.comment: Streaming SSR support with Angular SSR

partial-hydration.comment: Incremental hydration with @defer blocks; route-level render mode (v19+)

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Performance varies by version and signals usage; typically 1.3-1.6x baseline

component-libraries.comment: Angular Material, PrimeNG, NG-Zorro, NG Bootstrap, Nebular, Clarity, Ignite UI, Kendo UI

meta-framework.comment: Analog is the emerging meta-framework for Angular with Vite; Angular SSR built-in

major-companies-using.value: Google, Microsoft, IBM, Samsung, PayPal, Upwork, Forbes

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Maintained by dedicated team at Google; tested against Google's monorepo

job-market-demand.comment: Strong demand especially in enterprise; second to React globally

official-testing-library.comment: Angular Testing Module with TestBed for component testing

testing-library-name.comment: Vitest is default in v20+; Karma + Jasmine still supported

component-testing.comment: TestBed for component testing with dependency injection mocking

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright and Cypress; Protractor deprecated

mobile-framework.comment: Ionic for hybrid apps; NativeScript for truly native apps

pwa-support.comment: @angular/pwa package adds service worker and manifest

desktop-framework.comment: Works with Electron and Tauri for desktop apps

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve; requires understanding of TypeScript, DI, decorators, modules, RxJS, and many concepts

concepts-to-learn.comment: TypeScript, Components, Decorators, Modules, DI, Services, Templates, Directives, Pipes, Forms, Signals, RxJS, Routing, Guards, Lifecycle hooks

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes to scaffold and serve a new project with Angular CLI

migration-path.comment: ng update provides automated migrations between versions

incremental-adoption.comment: Full framework requiring significant commitment; not designed for sprinkling onto existing apps
